introSection: Fujiyama (Japanese: フジヤマ) is a TOGO hyper roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Fujiyama officially opened to the general public in July 1996.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.content: The Japanese for "Mount Fuji", 富士山, actually transliterates into Latin characters as "Fujisan"; "Fujiyama" results from a mistransliteration of 山, which can indeed be transliterated as "yama" in some contexts, but not in this context. It has been said that the name "Fujiyama" was given to this coaster as a deliberate joke on this common error.

fullText:
Fujiyama (Japanese: フジヤマ) is a TOGO hyper roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan.

Fujiyama officially opened to the general public in July 1996.



White track and black supports.



The Japanese for "Mount Fuji", 富士山, actually transliterates into Latin characters as "Fujisan"; "Fujiyama" results from a mistransliteration of 山, which can indeed be transliterated as "yama" in some contexts, but not in this context. It has been said that the name "Fujiyama" was given to this coaster as a deliberate joke on this common error.
